titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Charley Shively Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday August 19, 2020 at 03:55

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of writings, correspondence, research files, notebooks, printed material, photographs, family papers, and other papers documenting the life and work of Charley Shively. Writings, correspondence, and research files document both Shively's academic work as a historian and his work as a gay rights activist and publisher. Documentation of Shively's work as a publisher includes extensive materials related to the Boston-area gay newspaper Fag Rag, which Shively co-founded, including submissions, correspondence, and business records. Printed materials include many publications concerning the gay community and activism.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Charley Shively on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2013.
July 2015 Acquisition: Purchased from Elysium Press on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2015.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Charley Shively Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Charley Shively
bioghist.p: Charley Shively (1937-2017) was an American historian, publisher, poet, and gay rights activist.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: July 2013 Acquisition, July 2015 Acquisition.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Some materials in this collection were damaged by cat urine prior to receipt. Use caution when handling.
Boxes 94, 96, 113 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 92-93, 112 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.
Materials previously in Boxes 95 and 97 were deaccessioned in 2019. Boxes 95 and 97 no longer exist.
Some materials in this collection were damaged by cat urine prior to receipt. All materials were rehoused into new boxes, as well as folders when appropriate.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Charley Shively Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Charley Shively on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2013.

did.unittitle: Fag Rag correspondence and research files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Magazine articles, teaching files, and other materials. Contains: The Lordstown Struggle, and the Real Crisis in Production , by Ken Weller (Solidarity Pamphlet 145 - circa 1972); Solidarity , magazine (1977 May 13).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes a family geneology and other Charley Shively personal papers.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains research files for a dissertation and other files relating to various organization memberships.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of Whitman correspondence and other materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains material related to Fag Rag, specifically receipts for subscriptions, bills, general office correspondence, correspondence with Winston Leyland (publisher of Gay Sunshine and Gay Sunshine Press), and book material related to Braun-Brumfield Inc. Also contains: Directory of Short-Run Book Printers , by John Kremer (1985); Boss 5 (1979), edited by Reginald Gay.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original manuscripts, submitted to Fag Rag and other publications, on varying subjects relating to sexuality and revolution. Themes include group sex, public sex, matricide, incest, pederasty, beauty, love, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes works on history and poetry. Also includes subscriber and publisher bookkeeping.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains travel notes and "card poems."

did.unittitle: Side A - "Family History, Move to Las Vegas + Tricks" / Side B - "Trial FBI and Family History"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of audiovisual material may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Material related to Charley Shively's tenure at Boston State College

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a quantity of academic material, newspapers, offprints, correspondence, and photocopies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Stigma: Notes on the Management of Spoiled Identity , by Erving Goffman (1963). Also includes materials related to business expences.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Harvard University and high school yearbooks
A.J. MacDonald, Lysander Spooner, and Johnston Dale Allen research
Fag Rag materials, with related and personal correspondence

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Charley Shively's Harvard disertation research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Charley Shively's Harvard disertation research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes topics of sexuality, and sexual legal rights. Selected printed materials include The Intelligent Man's Guide to Handball (Sexual Sport) , Newslink Spring 1989, and PRO-ME-THEE-US , nos. 3 and 5.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Charley Shively's disertation research. Also includes correspondence and ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Charley Shively's disertation research and ephemera.

did.unittitle: Fag Rag files and related materials
Feminist, gay rights, and workers rights publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publications include Challenge 1999, Lesbian Connection Volume IV issue 3, The Spirit of Houston The First National Women's Conference , International Women and Health Resource Guide , Berkeley Barb November 1978, Achilles Heel a Magazine of Men's Politics Number 1 1978, Directory of Arts Activism , Mother Jones July 1978, Lilith Winter 1976, Intimacy , Se, race and Class by Selma Jones, Theme and Variations Aldebaran Review 18, Is Biology Woman's Destiny by Evelyn Reed, Women and Support Networks , Which Way for the Women's Movement by Cindy Jaquith and Willie Mae Reid, Seperated and Unequal , Papers on Patriarchy London 1976, Women, the Unions and Work by Selma James, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Bank records, and militant newspaper materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Calamus Lovers: Walt Whitman's Working Class Camerados, draft and proof
Gay liberation and anarchism newsletters, offprints, and periodicals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Gay liberation and anarchism newsletters, offprints, and periodicals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains both American and international gay newspapers and newsletters, including The Raddish .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes research on poetry, revolution, anarchy, gay liberation and other subjects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes travel materials, correspondence, writings, and radical and poetry publications. Significant titles include Porno Graphy by John Wilkson, Gay Thoughts by Byron Sokolik, Paper Frankenstein by Eve Wood, and Mad Dash Humbolt or If You Know It, It's Yours by the members of the ensemble Seachange.

did.unittitle: Writings, correspondence, and other materials related to Fag Rag and undergraduate work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a Fag Rag collage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files pertaining to Harvard University, Fullbright, travel, and teaching.

did.unittitle: Gay magazines, newsletters, and offprints, including Off Our Backs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes materials related to gay activism.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Significant titles include The Other (1967-1968), Guardian and Liberated Guardian (1969-1970), The Buddhist Third Class Junkmail Oracle (1970), Leviathan (1969), The Red Pencil (1969), and reprints of articles from the New England Free Press. Also includes newspaper clippings, and correspondence regarding subscriptions.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Freddie Greenfield papers and Fag Rag contributions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings, artwork, and correspondence. Significant titles include Stroker #6 and Assassin #4.

did.unittitle: Freddie Greenfield papers and Fag Rag contributions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings, artwork, and correspondence. Also contains photostat copies of Fag Rag: A Gay Male Newspaper , from June 1971 and Fall 1971.

did.unittitle: Freddie Greenfield papers and Fag Rag materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, writings, artwork and research relating to the publication of Fag Rag . Significant titles include two issues of Homosexual Counsling Journal , I'm Free by Bonnie Hoyum, Incisions volume 3, The Cafe Review spring 2002 and others.

did.unittitle: Cope Hope financial material and Freddie Greenfield correspondence

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of audiovisual materials may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Freddie Greenfield writings, sketches, and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains writings and Fag Rag correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an unpublished Charley Shively typescript. Also includes correspondence with H. Abelove, H. Allen, L. Anderson (Fag Rag); B. Barber (aka Billy Farant), Bifrost, Black Rose, S. Blazer, Jeb Boswell, Adrian Brooks, M. Bronski, P. Califia, Amy Clampett, K. Congdon, Ed Cox and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Demian, Gavin Dillard, Ed Dom, R. Duncan, photocopies to J. Weiners, Ken Dudley, Wayne Dynes, L. Eigner, C. Eshelman, J. Everhard and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes epehmera and printed materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes epehmera and printed materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes ephemera and printed materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes ephemera and printed materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes ephemera and printed materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes ephemera, printed materials and photographs of Charley Shively.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains various Walt Whitman materials, within a University of Iowa folder. Also contains Dialectics of Liberation, Susan Sherman, IKON, vol. 1, no. 3 (circa 1967).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains typescripts of Time Broken Hands, as well as hand-written "card poetry" notebooks. Also contains assorted print-outs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains hand-written "card poetry" notebooks. Also contains assorted print-outs.

did.unittitle: Correspondence related to Fag Rag, writings, financial papers, photographs, printed material, and other papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Charley Shively's master's thesis from the University of Wisconsin, "The Thought of Stephen Pearl Andrews (1812-1886)," 1960, and Osawatomie Weather Underground Organization , Vol. 2, No. 1, April-May 1976.

did.unittitle: The Future of Queer Publishing: Where Do We Go From Here?
#3 Rope by cop E.W. / #4 Mr. Miltz E.W.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes corrected drafts of Stonelick, Charley Shively's Harvard thesis "A History of the Conception of Death in America, 1650-1860," correspondence, and assorted printed material relating to gay liberation.

did.unittitle: Photographs, correspondence, juvenalia, and other materials
Academic files and graduate school notebooks

Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes The World World Language Catalog, photographs, photocopies, and negatives.

scopecontent.p: Also includes the NAMBLA Journal, typescripts, zines, periodicals, photocopies and notes. Significant titles include Pan: a magazine about boy-love , and Wounded Children , published by Christ is The Answer, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes the NAMBLA Journal , typescripts, zines, periodicals, photocopies and notes.

Side A: Walta reading for Charley, Discontinuos variation + Spicer + Roman 'BeeKeeper.' / Side B: DV poems; TE Lawrence, Thom Gunn, Elizabeth Bishop + Roman love poems for John
Side A: Walta reading stuff + M. Atwood / Side B:W. reading stuff + ? R

acqinfo.p: Purchased from Elysium Press on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2015.

did.unittitle: Juvenilia, personal papers, and writings by others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes material by Theodore Bruton and John Mitzel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes material from the Black Panthers, Free Peoples League, AIDs groups, and gay rights groups. Also includes Fag Rag correspondence, submissions, and travel material for Vietnam.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, writings and artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains paste-up mechanicals for The Fag Rag, as well as negative film sheets and original artwork. Includes multiple annotations by Charley Shively.

did.unittitle: Index cards and Harvard University reading room book slips
Portrait of young man [Gordon Copeland?], painting on particle board
Juvenilia, writings, research and printed materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Significant titles include Fuck (2001-2003), RFD , and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include "Oil Rig #99," "Cell Block #9," "Boots & Saddles," and "Sleaze."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a glass jar with various gay liberation-related pin-back buttons, and an American Legion Auxilliary patch.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of audiovisual materials may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: We Are Family Dr. Al Baird
Reclaiming the Past: The Difficult Task Of Queer History
